Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Iron King]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== The Captain ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 1''' — The Iron King is a ten-foot-tall fusion of man and machine.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 3''' — The Iron King is a being whose birth is being facilitated by a weapon aboard the Iron Hive.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 3''' — The Iron King has led the expansion of the Iron Legion and killed the narrator in a previous life.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 3''' — The Iron King is a fully mature entity that killed the protagonist in their first death.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 3''' — A torpedo contains the Iron King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 8''' — The Iron King was once unborn when the Absolute Burial spell was used on him.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 8''' — The Iron King once pierced Varic Vallenar's chest with his arm.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 18''' — The Iron King's consciousness was interrupted during the process of birth.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 18''' — The Iron King was sealed and his body launched into a star.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 18''' — The Legion has only managed to create an Iron King's mind four times in the past.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 18''' — The current Iron King is the fifth one created.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 18''' — The current Iron King is linked more closely to the Iron Legion than his ancestors.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 18''' — The Iron King is currently strapped to a steel table with most of his body being open circuits and exposed organs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 19''' — The Iron King is a figure within the Iron Legion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 20''' — The Iron King is the leader associated with the Iron Legion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 21''' — The Iron King was destroyed by the narrator in the Hive.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 28''' — An Iron King is highly intelligent.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 28''' — The Iron King is a masterwork blending of flesh and steel.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 28''' — The Iron King is ten feet tall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 28''' — The Iron King's eyes are burning portals of magical flame.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 28''' — The Iron King's skull is made of unbreakable glass.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 28''' — The Iron King is a mage with the signature magic to channel, augment, and control the powers of others.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 28''' — The Iron King expanded the Mirror of Silence spell a million times upon receiving it.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 30''' — An Iron King would want to take The Last Horizon for his own.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 31''' — An Iron King is a high-level entity within the Iron Legion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 34''' — The Iron King is an incomprehensible cyborg.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 34''' — The Iron King is hooked into the rest of the Legion via a network of veins and wires.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 34''' — The Iron King's presence is characterized by a ponderous weight and the temperature of a furnace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 35''' — An Iron King commanded a fleet that attacked The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 36''' — The Iron King is a person who may have provided information to the Galactic Union.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 37''' — Nelthan is terrified of the Iron King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 39''' — The Iron King is pushing into Galactic Union territory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 39''' — The Iron King has the processing power of the entire Legion to draw on.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 39''' — The Iron King's presence is so overwhelming that resistance against him is impossible.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 39''' — The Iron King once punched through the narrator's chest.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 40''' — The Iron King is an entity whose Grand Hive the crew plans to enter on a suicide mission.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 41''' — The Iron King has a detection range that the ship must dive past.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 42''' — The Iron King possesses fiery eyes and a magnification system built into his existence.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 42''' — The Iron King is a figure the narrator feared might seed the system with mines.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 43''' — The Iron King was caught off guard by the arrival of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 44''' — The Iron King left orders behind to sabotage ships from within using parasites.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 44''' — The Iron King has a head much larger than a human's with a brain covered in glass.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 45''' — The Iron King attempted to twist the Aether-connection of Eurias and force compatibility between his magic and the staff's power.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 45''' — The Iron King is connected to the Legion to an unprecedented and theoretically impossible degree.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 45''' — The Iron King cannot be overwhelmed mentally, physically, or exhausted magically.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 45''' — The Iron King released Eurias to fight the Divine Titan.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 46''' — The Iron King is a ten-foot-tall cyborg who weighs two tons.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 46''' — The Iron King was compressed to a point and forced out of reality by Absolute Burial.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 47''' — The Iron King can regain freedom through a mental connection to his Legion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 47''' — The Iron King's magic manifests as pink-and-orange flame.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 47''' — The Iron King's hand is a metal skeleton.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 47''' — The Iron King possesses Aether-disrupting magic in the form of pink flame.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 47''' — The Iron King can send out psionic pulses.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 48''' — The Iron King's brain was covered by a glass dome.}}

== The Engineer ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 3''' — The Iron King is a being that protections can be damaged in battle against.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 5''' — An Iron King was recently destroyed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 8''' — There are rumors of an Iron King leading the Iron Legion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 18''' — The Iron King was killed by the crew.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 21''' — Sola earned Units for killing the Iron King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 25''' — The Iron King is a being that could kill the Captain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 30''' — Iron Kings are entities that can kill people.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 32''' — The Iron King is not currently ready to be born.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 33''' — The Iron King is a figure Horizon fought.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 36''' — Omega claimed to have taken over the Iron King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 38''' — The Iron King spared the narrator's father.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 41''' — An Iron King in control of the entire Legion was able to capture Mell's father.}}

== The Knight ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 11''' — The Iron King can be searched for on the Subline.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 16''' — The Iron King is a figure Varic can attempt to locate using magic.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 16''' — The Iron King is a being created by summoning Esh’kinaar and the Iron Legion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 19''' — The Iron King made a bid for control of the galaxy.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 21''' — The Iron King showed Benri the scale of his magic.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 24''' — The Iron King is an enemy capable of hitting hard.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 24''' — The Iron King was killed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 24''' — The Iron King is a target that no weapon is too deadly to use against.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 39''' — The Iron King is a target that was previously hit with artillery.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 45''' — The Captain has dealt with the Iron King once before.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 45''' — The Iron King holds a high opinion of Benri's magic.}}

== The Pilot ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 2''' — The Iron King is a figure mentioned in relation to Benri's desire for guards.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 2''' — The Iron King is a mysterious entity that previously made Benri sign a magical contract.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 4''' — The Iron King did not kill the Captain's father.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 8''' — The Iron King was completely destroyed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 10''' — The narrator has been afraid of something since their battle with the Iron King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 20''' — The Perfected were attempting to create an Iron King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 22''' — The Iron King was the final Iron King in the galaxy.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 23''' — An Iron King has appeared for the first time in centuries.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 25''' — The data core contains research regarding the creation of the Iron King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 26''' — The Iron King is an apocalyptic enemy who has brought the narrator to the brink of death.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 32''' — Sola's crew includes people who can duel an Iron King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 38''' — The Iron King has a metal smile and visible brain that flickers like sparks of light.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 38''' — The Iron King stated that the game has not ended yet.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 39''' — The Iron Legion lacks an Iron King to coordinate its Hives.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 39''' — The Iron King is a figure Benri Vallenar has had a one-on-one conversation with.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 39''' — The Iron King is contained within an Aethertech box.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 39''' — The Iron King is a being that Benri Vallenar can give permission to use.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
